title: CLAUDE.md
text: file provides guidance Claude Code (claude.ai/code) working code repository.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: zip R package providing cross-platform ZIP compression without requiring external tools. wraps bundled miniz C library via R’s .Call() interface.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Install/build development: Run tests: Install (prefer uncovr::reload() iteration; install needed): R CMD check: Format R code (uses air): Regenerate README.md (README.Rmd):
code: # In R: uncovr::reload()      # load package in-place (fastest for iteration) uncovr::document()    # regenerate NAMESPACE and man/ from roxygen2 comments uncovr::test()                                           # all tests uncovr::test(filter = "zip")                             # filter by file name (no "test-" prefix) uncovr::install() withr::with_envvar(c(NOT_CRAN = "true"), rcmdcheck::rcmdcheck()) air format . make

title: CLAUDE.md
text: public R functions call C via .Call(). R layer handles argument validation path resolution; C layer actual compression/decompression. C entry points registered src/init.c: R_zip_zip, R_zip_unzip, R_zip_list, R_inflate, R_deflate. accessed R c_ prefix (e.g. c_R_zip_zip) .fixes = "c_" @useDynLib directive.
code: R functions (R/)   └── .Call() → C functions (src/rzip.c)                     └── miniz API (src/miniz.c, src/miniz.h)

title: CLAUDE.md
text: zip_internal() R/zip.R delegates path resolution get_zip_data() R/utils.R, builds data frame columns key (path inside zip), file (path disk), dir (logical). Two storage modes controlled keep_path: - mirror mode (keep_path = TRUE): preserves directory structure relative root - cherry-pick mode (keep_path = FALSE): top-level entry becomes root entry archive keys argument (explicit archive paths) bypasses modes via get_zip_data_keys().

title: CLAUDE.md
text: zip_process() unzip_process() R/process.R R6 classes (subclasses processx::process) run standalone command-line executables cmdzip cmdunzip. compiled src/tools/cmdzip.c src/tools/cmdunzip.c src/Makevars alongside shared library. zip_process serialises parameters binary file read cmdzip.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Windows, file paths go UTF-8 → UTF-16 conversion (zip__utf8_to_utf16 src/winutils.c) support long paths non-ASCII names. ZIP filenames without UTF-8 flag (bit 11 general purpose flag) treated IBM CP437 decoded UTF-8 via table src/zip.c. R helper enc2c() (R/utils.R) converts strings UTF-8 Windows native encoding elsewhere passing C.

title: Cross-Platform zip Compression
text: progress bars (see ), cli package needed. curl package needed using unzip() URLs. Background processes, .e. zip_process() unzip_process(), need processx package.

title: Cross-Platform zip Compression
code: library(zip)

title: Cross-Platform zip Compression
text: zip() creates new ZIP archive. (overwrites output file exists.) Simply supply directories files want include archive. makes sense change top-level directory files archiving , files stored using relative path name. Directories added recursively default. zip_append() similar zip(), appends files existing ZIP archive.
code: zip("sources.zip", c("R", "src")) file.info("sources.zip") #>               size isdir mode               mtime               ctime #> sources.zip 603127 FALSE  644 2025-01-07 10:40:54 2025-01-07 10:40:54 #>                           atime uid gid       uname grname #> sources.zip 2023-11-03 17:09:37 501  20 gaborcsardi  staff

title: Cross-Platform zip Compression
text: zip_list() lists files ZIP archive. returns data frame:

title: Cross-Platform zip Compression
text: unzip() uncompresses ZIP archive: unzip() zip_list() also work HTTP(S) URLs, without downloading whole file. needs curl package installed:
code: exdir <- tempfile() unzip("sources.zip", exdir = exdir) dir(exdir) #> [1] "R"   "src" zip_list("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/r-lib/zip/main/inst/example.zip") #> # A data frame: 4 × 8 #>   filename     compressed_size uncompressed_size timestamp           permissions #>   <chr>                  <dbl>             <dbl> <dttm>              <octmode>   #> 1 example/                   0                 0 2019-02-23 21:48:06 755         #> 2 example/dir/               0                 0 2019-02-23 21:49:36 755         #> 3 example/dir…              11                 6 2019-02-23 21:48:24 644         #> 4 example/fil…              11                 6 2019-02-23 21:48:28 644         #> # ℹ 3 more variables: crc32 <hexmode>, offset <dbl>, type <chr> unzip(   "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/r-lib/zip/main/inst/example.zip",   "example/file1",   exdir = exdir )

title: Cross-Platform zip Compression
text: Pass password argument encrypt entries WinZip AES-256 (default), supported 7-Zip, WinZip, macOS Archive Utility:
code: zip("secret.zip", "R", password = "hunter2") zip_list("secret.zip") #> # A data frame: 9 × 9 #>   filename     compressed_size uncompressed_size timestamp           permissions #>   <chr>                  <dbl>             <dbl> <dttm>              <octmode>   #> 1 R/                         0                 0 2026-06-09 10:32:24 755         #> 2 R/assertion…             178               388 2026-06-08 09:00:44 644         #> 3 R/compat-vc…            3415             14344 2026-06-08 09:00:44 644         #> 4 R/http.R                4893             16515 2026-06-08 12:10:24 644         #> 5 R/inflate.R              769              2390 2026-06-08 12:10:24 644         #> 6 R/process.R             2140              7474 2026-06-09 10:42:48 644         #> 7 R/utils.R               2249              7483 2026-06-09 10:27:50 644         #> 8 R/zip-packa…             127               122 2026-06-08 09:00:44 644         #> 9 R/zip.R                 5218             16564 2026-06-09 10:32:24 644         #> # ℹ 4 more variables: crc32 <hexmode>, offset <dbl>, type <chr>, #> #   encryption <chr> unzip("secret.zip", exdir = tempfile(), password = "hunter2")

what: Compressing and uncompressing in background processes
title: Cross-Platform zip Compression
text: can use zip_process() unzip_process() functions create background zip / unzip processes. processes implemented top processx::process class, pollable.

title: Cross-Platform zip Compression
text: zip() unzip() (co.) support progress bars cli package installed. disabled default (now), can enabled setting zip.progress option ZIP_PROGRESS environment variable TRUE.

title: Cross-Platform zip Compression
text: zip_password: default password ZIP encryption decryption. Can set string, raw vector, function returning one. zip.progress: set TRUE, progress bars enabled. Takes precedence ZIP_PROGRESS environment variable. zip_threads: number threads use threaded operations. Takes precedence ZIP_THREADS environment variable.

title: Cross-Platform zip Compression
text: R_ZIP_PROCESS_FALLBACK: set TRUE, unzip_process() functions fall back using R subprocess, without trying use cmdunzip executable embedded package. useful cmdunzip.exe blocked system policies Windows. ZIP_PROGRESS: set TRUE, progress bars enabled. zip.progress option takes precedence environment variable. ZIP_THREADS: number threads use threaded operations. zip_threads option takes precedence environment variable.

what: Compress a raw GZIP stream — deflate
title: Compress a raw GZIP stream — deflate
text: Compress raw GZIP stream

title: Compress a raw GZIP stream — deflate
code: deflate(buffer, level = 6L, pos = 1L, size = NULL)

title: Compress a raw GZIP stream — deflate
text: buffer Raw vector, containing data compress. level Compression level, integer 1 (fatest) 9 (best). pos Start position data compress buffer. size Compressed size estimate, NULL. given, small, output buffer resized multiple times.

title: Compress a raw GZIP stream — deflate
text: Named list three entries: output: raw vector, compressed data, bytes_read: number bytes used buffer, bytes_written: number bytes written output buffer.

title: Compress a raw GZIP stream — deflate
code: data_gz <- deflate(charToRaw("Hello world!")) inflate(data_gz$output) #> $output #>  [1] 48 65 6c 6c 6f 20 77 6f 72 6c 64 21 #>  #> $bytes_read #> [1] 24 #>  #> $bytes_written #> [1] 12 #>

what: Uncompress a raw GZIP stream — inflate
title: Uncompress a raw GZIP stream — inflate
text: Uncompress raw GZIP stream

title: Uncompress a raw GZIP stream — inflate
code: inflate(buffer, pos = 1L, size = NULL, raw = FALSE)

title: Uncompress a raw GZIP stream — inflate
text: buffer Raw vector, containing data uncompress. pos Start position data uncompress buffer. size Uncompressed size estimate, NULL. given, small, output buffer resized multiple times. raw Whether buffer contains raw DEFLATE stream, .e. one without zlib header trailer. default (FALSE) expects zlib stream.

title: Uncompress a raw GZIP stream — inflate
text: Named list three entries: output: raw vector, uncompressed data, bytes_read: number bytes used buffer, bytes_written: number bytes written output buffer.

title: Uncompress a raw GZIP stream — inflate
code: data_gz <- deflate(charToRaw("Hello world!")) inflate(data_gz$output) #> $output #>  [1] 48 65 6c 6c 6f 20 77 6f 72 6c 64 21 #>  #> $bytes_read #> [1] 24 #>  #> $bytes_written #> [1] 12 #>

title: Uncompress 'zip' Archives — unzip
text: unzip() always restores modification times extracted files directories.

title: Uncompress 'zip' Archives — unzip
code: unzip(   zipfile,   files = NULL,   overwrite = TRUE,   junkpaths = FALSE,   exdir = ".",   encoding = NULL,   password = NULL )

title: Uncompress 'zip' Archives — unzip
text: zipfile Path zip file uncompress, character vector paths. multiple paths given arguments defaults, files unzipped concurrently thread pool. Set zip_threads option ZIP_THREADS environment variable control number threads used. default zip uses two threads. files Character vector files extract archive. Files within directories can specified, must use forward slash path separator, zip files use internally. NULL, files extracted. overwrite Whether overwrite existing files. FALSE file already exists, error thrown. junkpaths Whether ignore directory paths creating files. TRUE, files created exdir. exdir Directory uncompress archive . exist, created. encoding Encoding use entry filenames. ZIP files signal UTF-8 filenames via flag entry; always decoded UTF-8 regardless encoding. entries without flag, encoding used; NULL (default) falls back IBM CP437, ZIP specification prescribes legacy entries. value passed iconv(). password Password decrypting encrypted entries. can string, raw vector, function returns one . NULL (default), zip_password option used, password also NULL. password silently ignored entries encrypted.

title: Uncompress 'zip' Archives — unzip
text: data frame one row per extracted entry columns, invisibly: filename (path within archive), compressed_size, uncompressed_size, timestamp, permissions, crc32, offset, type (zip_list()), path (absolute path extracted file disk).

title: Uncompress 'zip' Archives — unzip
text: zip archive stores permissions created Unix, permissions restored.

title: Uncompress 'zip' Archives — unzip
code: ## temporary directory, to avoid messing up the user's workspace. dir.create(tmp <- tempfile()) dir.create(file.path(tmp, "mydir")) cat("first file", file = file.path(tmp, "mydir", "file1")) cat("second file", file = file.path(tmp, "mydir", "file2"))  zipfile <- tempfile(fileext = ".zip") zip::zip(zipfile, "mydir", root = tmp)  ## List contents zip_list(zipfile) #> # A data frame: 3 × 9 #>   filename    compressed_size uncompressed_size timestamp           permissions #>   <chr>                 <dbl>             <dbl> <dttm>              <octmode>   #> 1 mydir/                    0                 0 2026-06-11 08:00:28 755         #> 2 mydir/file1              15                10 2026-06-11 08:00:28 644         #> 3 mydir/file2              16                11 2026-06-11 08:00:28 644         #> # ℹ 4 more variables: crc32 <hexmode>, offset <dbl>, type <chr>, #> #   encryption <chr>  ## Extract and inspect result tmp2 <- tempfile() result <- unzip(zipfile, exdir = tmp2) result[, c("filename", "path")] #> # A data frame: 3 × 2 #>   filename    path                                         #>   <chr>       <chr>                                        #> 1 mydir/      /tmp/RtmpCMfNY6/file1b8e1c3143f9/mydir/      #> 2 mydir/file1 /tmp/RtmpCMfNY6/file1b8e1c3143f9/mydir/file1 #> 3 mydir/file2 /tmp/RtmpCMfNY6/file1b8e1c3143f9/mydir/file2

what: Class for an external unzip process — unzip_process
title: Class for an external unzip process — unzip_process
text: unzip_process() returns R6 class represents unzip process. implemented subclass processx::process.

title: Class for an external unzip process — unzip_process
code: unzip_process()

title: Class for an external unzip process — unzip_process
text: unzip_process R6 class object, subclass processx::process, subclass callr::r_process fallback active (see Fallback section ).

title: Class for an external unzip process — unzip_process
text: See processx::process class methods. Arguments: zipfile: Path zip file uncompress. exdir: Directory uncompress archive . exist, created. poll_connection: passed initialize method processx::process, allows using processx::poll() poll_io() method poll completion process. stderr: passed initialize method processx::process, default standard error written temporary file. file can used diagnose errors process failed. ... passed initialize method processx::process.
code: up <- unzip_process()$new(zipfile, exdir = ".", poll_connection = TRUE,                            stderr = tempfile(), ...)

title: Class for an external unzip process — unzip_process
text: unzip_process() normally runs bundled cmdunzip native executable via processx::process. executable found fails self-test falls back running unzip() background R process via callr::r_process. may happen system policies allow starting cmdunzip executable., fallback class interface inherits callr::r_process instead processx::process. Set environment variable R_ZIP_PROCESS_FALLBACK=true force fallback unconditionally.

title: Class for an external unzip process — unzip_process
text: unzip_process class support encoding argument unzip(). Non-UTF-8 filenames decoded using IBM CP437 fallback. Use unzip() directly need handle ZIP files filenames encodings (e.g. CP932).

title: Class for an external unzip process — unzip_process
code: ex <- system.file("example.zip", package = "zip") tmp <- tempfile() up <- unzip_process()$new(ex, exdir = tmp) up$wait() up$get_exit_status() #> [1] 0 dir(tmp) #> [1] "example"

title: zip: Cross-Platform 'zip' Compression — zip-package
text: Cross-Platform 'zip' Compression Library. replacement 'zip' function, require additional external tools platform.

title: zip: Cross-Platform 'zip' Compression — zip-package
text: Maintainer: Gábor Csárdi csardi.gabor@gmail.com Authors: Gábor Csárdi csardi.gabor@gmail.com contributors: Kuba Podgórski [contributor] Rich Geldreich [contributor] Arm Limited (bundled Mbed TLS crypto subset src/mbedtls/ (Apache-2.0)) [contributor, copyright holder] Posit Software, PBC (ROR) [copyright holder, funder]

title: Compress Files into 'zip' Archives — zip
text: zip() creates new zip archive file.

title: Compress Files into 'zip' Archives — zip
code: zip(   zipfile,   files,   recurse = TRUE,   compression_level = 9,   include_directories = TRUE,   root = ".",   mode = c("mirror", "cherry-pick"),   keys = NULL,   password = NULL,   encryption = c("aes256", "aes128", "zipcrypto") )  zipr(   zipfile,   files,   recurse = TRUE,   compression_level = 9,   include_directories = TRUE,   root = ".",   mode = c("cherry-pick", "mirror"),   keys = NULL,   password = NULL,   encryption = c("aes256", "aes128", "zipcrypto") )  zip_append(   zipfile,   files,   recurse = TRUE,   compression_level = 9,   include_directories = TRUE,   root = ".",   mode = c("mirror", "cherry-pick"),   keys = NULL,   password = NULL,   encryption = c("aes256", "aes128", "zipcrypto") )  zipr_append(   zipfile,   files,   recurse = TRUE,   compression_level = 9,   include_directories = TRUE,   root = ".",   mode = c("cherry-pick", "mirror"),   keys = NULL,   password = NULL,   encryption = c("aes256", "aes128", "zipcrypto") )

title: Compress Files into 'zip' Archives — zip
text: zipfile zip file create. file exists, zip overwrites , zip_append appends . directory error thrown. files Character vector paths files add archive. See details absolute relative path names. recurse Whether add contents directories recursively. compression_level number 1 9. 9 compresses best, also takes longest. include_directories Whether explicitly include directories archive. Including directories might confuse MS Office reading docx files, set FALSE creating . root Change working directory creating archive. mode Selects files directories stored archive. can "mirror" "cherry-pick". See "Relative Paths" details. keys optional character vector length files, specifying paths corresponding entries inside zip archive. file, key exact archive path. directory, key becomes directory prefix contents stored. NULL (default), paths determined mode. "." may appear files keys specified. password Password encrypting archive entries. can string, raw vector bytes, zero-argument function returns one . NULL (default), zip_password option consulted; also NULL, entries stored unencrypted. password interpreted UTF-8 bytes regardless current locale, matches WinZip/7-Zip convention ensures interoperability across platforms. encryption Encryption scheme use password NULL. "aes256" (default) "aes128" use WinZip AES encryption (AES-256 AES-128 CTR mode, key derived via PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA1, HMAC-SHA1 authentication tag). scheme supported 7-Zip, WinZip, macOS Archive Utility. "zipcrypto" uses legacy PKWARE ZipCrypto stream cipher, cryptographically weak used compatibility tools support AES encryption.

title: Compress Files into 'zip' Archives — zip
text: name created zip file, invisibly.

title: Compress Files into 'zip' Archives — zip
text: zip_append() appends compressed files existing 'zip' file.

title: Compress Files into 'zip' Archives — zip
text: zip() zip_append() can run two different modes: mirror mode cherry picking mode. handle specified files differently.

title: Compress Files into 'zip' Archives — zip
text: Mirror mode creating zip archive directory structure, exactly disk. current working directory root archive, paths fully kept. zip changes current directory root creating archive. E.g. consider following directory structure:   Assuming current working directory foo, following zip entries created zip:   Note zip refuses store files absolute paths, chops leading / character file names. relative paths allowed zip files.
code: . |-- foo |   |-- bar |   |   |-- file1 |   |   `-- file2 |   `-- bar2 `-- foo2     `-- file3 setwd("foo") zip::zip("../test.zip", c("bar/file1", "bar2", "../foo2")) #> Warning in warn_for_dotdot(data$key): Some paths reference parent directory, #> creating non-portable zip file zip_list("../test.zip")[, "filename", drop = FALSE] #> # A data frame: 4 x 1 #>   filename #>   <chr> #> 1 bar/file1 #> 2 bar2/ #> 3 ../foo2/ #> 4 ../foo2/file3

title: Compress Files into 'zip' Archives — zip
text: cherry picking mode, selected files directories root archive. mode handy want select subset files directories, possibly different paths put archive, top level. example directory structure :   zip version 2.3.0, "." special meaning files argument: include files (possibly directories) within current working directory, working directory . Note applies cherry picking mode.
code: zip::zip(   "../test2.zip",   c("bar/file1", "bar2", "../foo2"),   mode = "cherry-pick" ) zip_list("../test2.zip")[, "filename", drop = FALSE] #> # A data frame: 4 x 1 #>   filename #>   <chr> #> 1 file1 #> 2 bar2/ #> 3 foo2/ #> 4 foo2/file3

title: Compress Files into 'zip' Archives — zip
text: zip() (zip_append(), etc.) add permissions archived files directories ZIP archive, Unix systems. zip unzip implementations support , recovered extracting archive. Note, however owner group (uid gid) currently omitted, even Unix.

title: Compress Files into 'zip' Archives — zip
text: functions exist historical reasons. identical zip() zip_append() different default mode argument.

title: Compress Files into 'zip' Archives — zip
code: ## Some files to zip up. We will run all this in the R session's ## temporary directory, to avoid messing up the user's workspace. dir.create(tmp <- tempfile()) dir.create(file.path(tmp, "mydir")) cat("first file", file = file.path(tmp, "mydir", "file1")) cat("second file", file = file.path(tmp, "mydir", "file2"))  zipfile <- tempfile(fileext = ".zip") zip::zip(zipfile, "mydir", root = tmp)  ## List contents zip_list(zipfile) #> # A data frame: 3 × 9 #>   filename    compressed_size uncompressed_size timestamp           permissions #>   <chr>                 <dbl>             <dbl> <dttm>              <octmode>   #> 1 mydir/                    0                 0 2026-06-11 08:00:28 755         #> 2 mydir/file1              15                10 2026-06-11 08:00:28 644         #> 3 mydir/file2              16                11 2026-06-11 08:00:28 644         #> # ℹ 4 more variables: crc32 <hexmode>, offset <dbl>, type <chr>, #> #   encryption <chr>  ## Add another file cat("third file", file = file.path(tmp, "mydir", "file3")) zip_append(zipfile, file.path("mydir", "file3"), root = tmp) zip_list(zipfile) #> # A data frame: 4 × 9 #>   filename    compressed_size uncompressed_size timestamp           permissions #>   <chr>                 <dbl>             <dbl> <dttm>              <octmode>   #> 1 mydir/                    0                 0 2026-06-11 08:00:28 755         #> 2 mydir/file1              15                10 2026-06-11 08:00:28 644         #> 3 mydir/file2              16                11 2026-06-11 08:00:28 644         #> 4 mydir/file3              15                10 2026-06-11 08:00:28 644         #> # ℹ 4 more variables: crc32 <hexmode>, offset <dbl>, type <chr>, #> #   encryption <chr>

what: List Files in a 'zip' Archive — zip_list
title: List Files in a 'zip' Archive — zip_list
text: List Files 'zip' Archive

title: List Files in a 'zip' Archive — zip_list
code: zip_list(zipfile, encoding = NULL)

title: List Files in a 'zip' Archive — zip_list
text: zipfile Path existing ZIP file. encoding Encoding use entry filenames. ZIP files signal UTF-8 filenames via flag entry; always decoded UTF-8 regardless encoding. entries without flag, encoding used; NULL (default) falls back IBM CP437, ZIP specification prescribes legacy entries. value passed iconv().

title: List Files in a 'zip' Archive — zip_list
text: data frame columns: filename, compressed_size, uncompressed_size, timestamp, permissions, crc32, offset, type encryption. type one file, block_device, character_device, directory, FIFO, symlink socket. encryption one none, aes128, aes192, aes256, zipcrypto, NA encrypted scheme determined.

title: List Files in a 'zip' Archive — zip_list
text: Note crc32 formatted using .hexmode(). offset refers start local zip header entry. Following approach seek() stored numeric rather integer vector can therefore represent values 2^53-1 (9 PB).

title: Class for an external zip process — zip_process
text: See processx::process class methods. Arguments: zipfile: Path zip file create. files: Character vector paths files add archive. specified file directory created top-level entry zip archive. recurse: Whether add contents directories recursively. include_directories: Whether explicitly include directories archive. Including directories might confuse MS Office reading docx files, set FALSE creating . poll_connection: passed initialize method processx::process, allows using processx::poll() poll_io() method poll completion process. stderr: passed initialize method processx::process, default standard error written temporary file. file can used diagnose errors process failed. ... passed initialize method processx::process.
code: zp <- zip_process()$new(zipfile, files, recurse = TRUE,                          poll_connection = TRUE,                          stderr = tempfile(), ...)

title: Class for an external zip process — zip_process
code: dir.create(tmp <- tempfile()) write.table(iris, file = file.path(tmp, "iris.ssv")) zipfile <- tempfile(fileext = ".zip") zp <- zip_process()$new(zipfile, tmp) zp$wait() zp$get_exit_status() #> [1] 0 zip_list(zipfile) #> # A data frame: 2 × 9 #>   filename     compressed_size uncompressed_size timestamp           permissions #>   <chr>                  <dbl>             <dbl> <dttm>              <octmode>   #> 1 file1b8e438…               0                 0 2026-06-11 08:00:30 755         #> 2 file1b8e438…            1126              4818 2026-06-11 08:00:30 644         #> # ℹ 4 more variables: crc32 <hexmode>, offset <dbl>, type <chr>, #> #   encryption <chr>

title: zip 3.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-10 zip(), zipr(), zip_append(), zipr_append(), zip_process(), unzip() / unzip_process() now support password-protected archives using WinZIP AES-256, encryption schemes (#38). zip_list() now reports encryption column indicating encryption scheme used entry (#38). unzip() now vectorized. arguments apart zipfile, exdir password default, unprocesses files concurrently, using thread pool. size thread pool can set zip_threads option ZIP_THREADS environment variable (#147). zip_list() unzip() now work directly http:// https:// URLs. use HTTP range requests download central directory requested entries, listing extracting files large remote archive downloads whole file. server support range requests, fall back downloading entire archive (warning). requires curl package (#39). zip_list() unzip() now report Unix permission bits stored archive Windows well. Previously always reported 700/600 Windows, regardless permissions recorded ZIP file. zip_list() unzip() now report type == "directory" directory entries whose Unix mode bits lack S_IFDIR marked directories trailing slash DOS directory attribute (e.g. archives created zip() ). Previously reported "file". zip() unzip() now show progress bar cli package installed. zip(), progress byte-level, large single files tracked smoothly. unzip(), progress advances per extracted entry. Progress bars (now) opt-via ZIP_PROGRESS=true environment variable zip.progress option (#48). unzip() now returns data frame (invisibly) one row per extracted entry, containing columns zip_list() (filename, compressed_size, uncompressed_size, timestamp, permissions, crc32, offset, type) plus path column absolute path extracted file disk (#35). zip_list() unzip() now encoding argument ZIP files non-UTF-8, non-CP437 filenames (e.g. CP932/Shift-JIS Japanese Windows). encoding set, filenames without UTF-8 flag decoded specified code page instead CP437 (#101). zip_append() zipr_append() now replace existing entries appending file whose archive path already exists zip file, instead creating duplicate entries (#111). unzip() zip_list() now correctly handle ZIP files non-UTF-8 filenames (e.g. filenames encoded IBM CP437, created many Windows tools). filenames converted UTF-8 using CP437 character map UTF-8 flag set ZIP entry (#103). New keys argument zip(), zipr(), zip_append(), zipr_append(). allows specifying custom paths entries inside archive, independently paths disk (#50). unzip_process() now probes falls back using R subprocess bundled cmdunzip executable started (#135). Updated embedded miniz version 3.1.1 (#122).

title: zip 2.3.3
text: CRAN release: 2025-05-13 zip_list() now type column, file type. unzip() now correctly creates symbolic links Unix (#127).

title: zip 2.3.2
text: CRAN release: 2025-02-01 zip_list() now returns tbl object, loads pillar package, installed, produce nicer output long data frames.

title: zip 2.3.1
text: CRAN release: 2024-01-27 zip shared library now hides symbols (platforms support ), avoid name clashes libraries (#98).

title: zip 2.3.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-04-17 zip now handles large zip files Windows (#65, #75, #79, @weshinsley). zip now behaves better absolute paths mirror mode, paths contain : character (#69, #70). zip::unzip() now uses process’s umask value (see umask(2)) Unix zip file contain Unix permissions (#67). Fix segmentation fault zip file can’t created (#91, @zeehio) Fix delayed evaluation error zipfile zip::zip() used (#92, @zeehio) New deflate() inflate() functions compress uncompress GZIP streams memory.

title: zip 2.2.2
